api.title: API Reference
api.intro: The reference below comes from the manifest generated from annotated Opus sources.
bullets: A domain groups the classes detected in the manifest.
Each symbol links back to its source and public methods.
Editorial descriptions complement the manifest without replacing source evidence.
Navigation does not depend on .htaccess: links use ?page=...

symbol.fallback_role: Source role to enrich in OPUS_REFBOOK tags.
symbol.no_contract: Source contract to enrich in OPUS_REFBOOK tags.

search.title: Search
search.intro: Quickly find a class, method, domain, guide or Opus framework file.
search.placeholder_long: Example: Router, ACL, render, config, ScoreTemplate…
search.help: Search scans indexed guides, domains, symbols, namespaces, files, contracts and public methods.
search.empty_body: Try a shorter name, a domain such as ACL or Database, or a known public method.
tips: A class or interface: Router, ViewModel, AccessControl.
A public method: render, decide, fromXml, dispatch.
A domain: ACL, Database, Renderer, Template, HTTP.
A file, namespace or contract term.
search.snippet_default: Match found in the RefBook catalog.

footer.license_summary: Free for personal, educational and non-commercial use. Professional, commercial, SaaS, redistribution or product integration use requires a commercial license.

runtime.title: FSM-driven internal API
runtime.asset_warning_body: The Opus snapshot references missing examples or diagrams. RefBook shows diagnostics without inventing replacement content.

assets.title: Documentation asset diagnostics
assets.intro: This page turns missing references into an action plan. RefBook does not generate replacement content.
assets.truth_suffix: Files to create belong to the shared Opus framework, not to RefBook.
assets.no_placeholder: Do not generate silent placeholders in RefBook.
assets.rerun_smoke: Run the API smoke again after each asset batch.

legal.title: Opus Framework · source-available · dual licensed
legal.intro: Opus Framework is publicly documented through this RefBook. The RefBook is a public documentation surface; it is not the downloadable distribution package.
legal.personal_free: Free for personal, educational and non-commercial use.
legal.commercial_required: Professional, commercial, SaaS, redistribution or product integration use requires a commercial license.
legal.rights_reserved: All rights reserved except as expressly granted by the applicable Opus license.
legal.note: This page describes the public licensing position displayed by the RefBook. The final downloadable Opus package will carry the detailed license files appropriate to its distribution channel.
legal.distribution_body_1: The RefBook is intended to be publicly accessible as documentation and discoverable by search engines.
legal.distribution_body_2: The RefBook source repository may remain private. Opus itself is the framework intended for future package distribution.

symbol_extra.declared_examples_missing: References declared, but assets are not available in the snapshot.
symbol_extra.declared_diagrams_missing: References declared, but diagrams are not available in the snapshot.

seo.description_default: Opus Framework Reference Book: public technical documentation, contracts, classes, diagrams and examples for the Opus PHP framework.
seo.description_legal: Opus Framework source-available dual licensing, copyright and author attribution.
seo.description_api_reference: Opus Reference Book internal API documentation, runtime snapshot and framework domain map.
seo.description_download_install: Opus Framework download and installation guidance for the public distribution channel.
seo.description_asset_diagnostics: RefBook diagnostics for missing documentation examples and diagrams.
seo.description_search: Search the Opus Reference Book guides, domains, symbols and public methods.
seo.description_domain_suffix: domain reference: classes, contracts, runtime behavior and examples.

module.title: OPUS FRAMEWORK
module.subtitle: Reference Book
module.mission: Make Opus readable, verifiable, and usable without digging through code at every step.

home_cards.title: Read fast
home_cards.body: The left column exposes domains and guides. The API page groups detected symbols.

home_cards.title: Verify
home_cards.body: Every domain and every symbol comes from the manifest generated from <OPUS_ROOT>.

home_cards.title: Develop
home_cards.body: Every change must stay MVC: thin controller, explicit service, ViewModel, clean ScoreTemplate.

home_cards.title: Shorter menu
home_cards.body: Guides and domains are grouped into collapsible panels to keep navigation readable.

home_cards.title: Richer pages
home_cards.body: Domain pages now show classes, interfaces, public methods and source inventory.

guides.title: Architecture overview
guides.summary: Opus separates application, routing, control, service, rendering, security, and configuration.

sections.title: Functional reading
items: SiteResolver selects the current site configuration.
Router turns a URL into a RouteMatch.
ControllerDispatcher instantiates the controller and triggers the action.
ViewModel carries data to the renderer.
ScoreTemplateRenderer turns the view into HTML.

sections.title: Maintenance rule
items: Do not put business logic in public/index.php.
Do not use ScoreTemplate as data storage.
Do not bypass the dispatcher to render a page.
Do not edit server configuration to fix an application issue.

sections.title: Key takeaways
items: One responsibility must belong to one layer.
A service prepares data; ScoreTemplate must not invent it.
A contract error must be explicit and traceable.

guides.reading: Overview for deciding where a responsibility belongs before changing Opus.

guides.title: Opus MVC contract
guides.summary: Official route, thin controller, business service, ViewModel, template.

sections.title: Service
items: Does not depend on the browser.

sections.title: Correct flow
items: The renderer transforms the ViewModel without business logic.

guides.reading: Daily working contract: the route finds the controller, the controller prepares, ScoreTemplate renders.

guides.title: OPUS_REFBOOK source tags
guides.summary: Official API content starts from annotated Opus classes and then passes through a controlled manifest.

sections.title: Goal
items: Describe the role of a class.

sections.title: Rule
items: A generated page must trace back to a source symbol.
Editorial text complements the symbol; it does not invent it.
A source/documentation mismatch must produce a source or manifest correction.

sections.title: Progressive population
items: Each new source annotation enriches the manifest.
Editorial text may explain, not replace source evidence.
An incomplete symbol must remain visibly incomplete.

guides.reading: Starting point for the generated Reference Book: annotated source remains the evidence.

guides.title: Routing and front controller
guides.summary: Apache serves public/; Opus receives the request; the router selects the route.

sections.title: Quick diagnostic
items: Check the target controller before Apache.

guides.reading: Helps diagnose a page without moving the problem to Apache or .htaccess.

guides.title: LSTSA — Load Store Transform Secure Archive
guides.summary: LSTSA loads, validates, transforms, stores, logs, and archives typed transformations.

sections.title: Steps
items: Store: write to the configured target.
Secure: validate types, lengths, and constraints.
Archive: keep an archivable log per transformation.

sections.title: Rules
items: Configuration describes source, target, types, and lengths.
Each run produces a report and a dedicated log.

sections.title: Logging
items: Each transformation owns a dedicated log.
Type or length refusals must state the data and rule.

guides.reading: Framework for transforming typed, controlled and archivable data.

guides.title: Tests and recipes
guides.summary: An Opus milestone must be validated by lint, smoke, targeted recipe, and clean Git state.

sections.title: Order
items: HTTP test when required by the milestone.

sections.title: Forbidden
items: No broad global refactor unless requested.
No Apache modification to fix an application page.

guides.reading: Minimum recipe before considering a step deliverable.
